title: [lichen_l2] replay_table not cleared in enable disable cycle
description:
Cross-file state machine bug: lichen_l2_enable(false) clears replay_table (line 786), but if enable(true) fails at lichen_lora_l2_start() (line 729), the rollback path clears link_ctx_initialized but does NOT clear replay_table.

State inconsistency:
1. Interface running, replay_table has peer windows
2. lichen_l2_enable(false) called - clears link_ctx, replay_table, peer_table
3. lichen_l2_enable(true) called
4. link_ctx_initialized set to 1, replay_table re-init (line 716)
5. lichen_lora_l2_start() fails (e.g., lora_config returns error)
6. Rollback sets link_ctx_initialized=0, calls lichen_link_cleanup()
7. But replay_table is left initialized with empty windows

This is actually NOT a bug - the replay_table being initialized with empty windows is fine for the next enable(true) attempt. The init path doesn't depend on replay_table being in any particular state.

Actually, re-reading: the rollback at lines 739-746 does NOT call lichen_replay_table_init(). But since replay_table was just initialized at line 716, it's already empty. No actual bug here.

Cancel this - not a real bug.
